question: What is the minimum age requirement to participate in the plan?
answer: You are eligible to participate in the Plan if you are at least 21 years of age [Page 2].
ground_truth: You must be at least 21 years of age.

question: How many hours of service do I need to be eligible?
answer: You need to have completed 1,000 hours in a 12-month period to be eligible for the Plan [Page 2].
ground_truth: You need 1,000 hours of service in a 12-month period.

question: Are temporary employees eligible for the plan?
answer: Temporary employees are not eligible to participate in the Plan [Page 2].
ground_truth: No, temporary employees and independent contractors are not eligible.

question: What defines a year of service for eligibility?
answer: A year of service for eligibility is defined as completing 1,000 hours in a 12-month period [Page 2].
ground_truth: One year of service is defined as 1,000 hours worked in a 12-month period.

question: Who is eligible to participate in the ACME 401(k) plan?
answer: You are eligible to participate in the Plan if you are at least 21 years of age, have completed one year of service (1,000 hours in a 12-month period), and are classified as a common-law employee of ACME Corporation [Page 2]. Temporary employees and independent contractors are not eligible [Page 2].
ground_truth: Common-law employees who are at least 21 years old and have completed one year of service.

question: What is the maximum percentage I can contribute to the plan?
answer: You may contribute up to 75% of your eligible compensation on a pre-tax basis (traditional 401(k)) or after-tax basis (Roth 401(k)) [Page 3].
ground_truth: You may contribute between 1% and 75% of your eligible compensation.

question: What is the IRS contribution limit for 2024?
answer: The IRS contribution limit for 2024 is $23,000, and if you are age 50 or older, you may make catch-up contributions of an additional $7,500 [Page 3].
ground_truth: The IRS contribution limit for 2024 is $23,000, with an additional $7,500 catch-up for age 50+.

question: What is the employer matching contribution formula?
answer: The employer matching contribution is equal to 100% of your deferrals up to 3% of compensation, plus 50% of your deferrals from 3% to 5% of compensation [Page 4]. The maximum employer match is 4% of eligible compensation [Page 4].
ground_truth: 100% of deferrals up to 3% of compensation, plus 50% of deferrals from 3% to 5% of compensation, with a maximum match of 4%.

question: Does the plan offer Roth 401(k) contributions?
answer: The plan offers contributions on an after-tax basis, which is referred to as Roth 401(k) [Page 3]. You may contribute between 1% and 75% of your eligible compensation on a pre-tax basis or after-tax basis, subject to IRS limits [Page 3].
ground_truth: Yes, you can contribute on an after-tax basis (Roth 401(k)).

question: How much catch-up contribution can I make if I'm over 50?
answer: If you are age 50 or older, you may make catch-up contributions of an additional $7,500 [Page 3].
ground_truth: An additional $7,500 in catch-up contributions.

question: What is the vesting schedule for employer matching contributions?
answer: The vesting schedule for employer matching contributions follows a 3-year cliff schedule, where you are 0% vested for less than 1, 1, and 2 years of service, and 100% vested after 3 or more years of service [Page 5]. If you leave before completing 3 years of service, you will forfeit all employer matching contributions [Page 5].
ground_truth: 3-year cliff vesting: 0% for less than 3 years of service, 100% after 3 years.

question: Am I vested in my own employee contributions?
answer: You are always 100% vested in your own contributions [Page 5].
ground_truth: Yes, you are always 100% vested in your own contributions.

question: What happens to unvested employer contributions if I leave?
answer: If you leave ACME Corporation, any unvested employer contributions will be forfeited [Page 7]. You will receive only your vested account balance when you leave the company [Page 7].
ground_truth: Any unvested employer contributions will be forfeited.

question: How many years of service do I need to be fully vested in employer contributions?
answer: You need 3 or more years of service to be fully vested in employer matching contributions, as this is a 3-year cliff vesting schedule [Page 5]. If you leave before completing 3 years of service, you will forfeit all employer matching contributions [Page 5].
ground_truth: 3 or more years of service for 100% vesting.

question: What are my options if I leave ACME Corporation?
answer: If you leave ACME Corporation, you have several options: you can leave your money in the Plan if your balance is more than $7,000, roll over to an IRA or another employer's qualified plan, or take a lump-sum distribution which is subject to taxes and penalties [Page 7]. You will receive only your vested account balance, and any unvested employer contributions will be forfeited [Page 7].
ground_truth: Leave money in the plan (if balance > $7,000), roll over to IRA or another plan, or take lump-sum distribution.

question: Can I take a hardship withdrawal for medical expenses?
answer: You may request a hardship withdrawal for medical expenses for you or your dependents [Page 6]. However, you must exhaust all available plan loans before requesting a hardship withdrawal [Page 6]. Additionally, hardship withdrawals are subject to income tax and may incur a 10% early withdrawal penalty if you are under age 59½ [Page 6].
ground_truth: Yes, medical expenses for you or your dependents qualify for hardship withdrawal.

question: At what age can I take in-service withdrawals?
answer: You can take in-service withdrawals from your account while still employed at age 59½, subject to income tax [Page 8].
ground_truth: At age 59½, you may take in-service withdrawals while still employed.

question: When do Required Minimum Distributions begin?
answer: Required Minimum Distributions (RMDs) begin at age 73 if you are still employed [Page 8]. However, if you own more than 5% of ACME Corporation, RMDs begin at age 73 regardless of your employment status [Page 8].
ground_truth: RMDs begin at age 73 if still employed, or regardless of employment if you own more than 5% of the company.
